note: saber.sa resolves as official SASO-operated platform (confirmed 2026-06-11: HTTPS, .sa domain, Digital Government Authority reg no. 20250414462, 5.59M+ registered products). PCoC + per-shipment SCoC mandatory requirement confirmed by multiple independent sources and 2025 regulation updates (undertaking letters abolished Jan 2025; post-arrival SCoC applications invalid). mandatory_status 'mandatory' correct. Gap description accurate — no Chinese equivalent pre-export registration exists. Row is appropriately tagged throughout. No source URL fix needed.

saso.gov.sa resolves as official SASO government site (confirmed 2026-06-11: Arabic primary, DGA reg 20250414141). SASO has published a Technical Regulation for Solar Photovoltaic Systems (PDF confirmed at saso.gov.sa/en/Laws-And-Regulations/Technical_regulations/Documents/TR-Solar-Photovoltaic-Systems-Version-2.pdf) which references IEC 62109. Real-world SASO IEC 62109-1 certificates exist (e.g., Deye cert no. 20240605403). IECEE SIRC pathway for SABER PCoC confirmed. mandatory_status 'mandatory' correct. Gap note about CB recognition and edition acceptability is accurate and appropriately hedged. Edition numbers for SASO adoption remain unconfirmed — tag correct. No source URL fix needed.

saso.gov.sa resolves (confirmed). SASO EMC Technical Regulation confirmed: published 2023-11-17, effective 2024-05-15 (some sources say May 17). Covers broad electrical products including battery chargers, power supplies — inverters/power converters for PV are in scope by product type (HS-code scope lists 46 product categories). Compliance route: SDoC + accredited EMC test reports submitted via SABER PCoC application — consistent with gap description. Specific SASO regulation reference/number not publicly surfaced in open search; row correctly omits a specific number and marks . mandatory_status 'mandatory' correct. Gap that CE EMC reports may be accepted as supporting evidence is plausible and consistent with SABER SDoC pathway, but requires CB confirmation — tag appropriate. No source URL fix needed.

Source URL https://www.se.com.sa resolves (confirmed 2026-06-11) as Saudi Energy (SE), formerly Saudi Electricity Company (SEC). IMPORTANT STALE CONTENT FLAGS: (1) Saudi Electricity Company officially rebranded as 'Saudi Energy (SE)' in February 2026 — publisher field should eventually read 'Saudi Energy (SE)'; URL remains correct. (2) 'WERA' referenced in requirement_topic and target_requirement.summary is stale: WERA was renamed to SERA (Saudi Electricity Regulatory Authority) by Saudi Cabinet on 2024-05-07 (confirmed via sera.gov.sa). The canonical regulatory authority for grid code is now SERA at https://www.sera.gov.sa. The Saudi Arabian Grid Code is confirmed in SERA/WERA documents (May 2024 update confirmed at sera.gov.sa). 60 Hz grid fact is correct — Saudi national grid operates at 60 Hz. Separate SABER vs. grid-access compliance distinction is accurate. Gap description is substantively correct. mandatory_status 'mandatory' correct. These are content-text errors, not source URL errors; source URL is not dead. No URL fix applied (per instructions, only source.url is fixable). Recommend human review to update 'WERA' references to 'SERA' in content text and add sera.gov.sa as additional source.

saso.gov.sa resolves (confirmed). Arabic labelling mandatory for Saudi Arabia confirmed by US Government trade.gov country guide and multiple independent sources: all imported goods for distribution must be labelled in Arabic (at minimum Arabic + English bilingual is permitted); safety/warning text must be in Arabic. mandatory_status 'mandatory' correct. Gap (new Arabic labels required) accurate. SEEC energy efficiency label for PV inverters: searched SEEC website — PV inverters do NOT appear in current mandatory SEEC product scope (confirmed products include dishwashers from Jan 2025; no solar inverter energy label programme found). Row correctly hedges SEEC applicability with — this is the appropriate stance. SASO Arabic label regulation specific reference number unconfirmed in open search; tag appropriate. No source URL fix needed.
